page: Microsoft Azure EC16ads_cc_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the AMD EPYC 7763 processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this server features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The processor runs at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz and is supported by 32 KB L1d cache, 0.5 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU.

The instance includes 614 GB of local disk storage and is Premium Storage capable. In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance, scoring 2701.89 in stress-ng single-core tests, which places it in the top 25% of tested hardware. Multi-core performance is average, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 21606.41 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 9859.0. Memory bandwidth is stable, recording 40.12 GB/sec for large-block read-only operations and 25.93 GB/sec for large-block write operations.

For specialized workloads, the server achieves a strong text generation speed of 276.68 tokens/sec during LLM inference with a small 135M model, though prompt processing for the same model remains average at 967.56 tokens/sec. Database operations and key-value store performance are solid, with Redis SET operations reaching 15.84 million operations per second. Given its high memory density and local storage capacity, this instance offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive applications, relational databases, and data processing tasks that require substantial RAM without the overhead of dedicated GPU hardware.
description: Microsoft Azure EC16ads_cc_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz. This Hyper-V-based instance includes 614 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core CPU capabilities, with a stress-ng single-core score of 2701.89, alongside average multi-core and memory bandwidth results. The server excels in text generation for small language models, reaching 276.68 tokens/sec. With a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, this instance is qualitatively cost-effective for memory-heavy workloads, database operations, and caching layers like Redis, which registered 15.84 million SET operations per second. It is well-suited for relational databases, memory-intensive data processing, and web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure EC16ads_cc_v5 is a memory-optimized VM with 16 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 614 GB local storage, powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 CPU for database and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure EC16ads_cc_v5 is a memory-optimized VM featuring 16 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 614 GB local storage on an AMD EPYC 7763 processor.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing, high RAM density, and fast local storage for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 16-vCPU AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz
Equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM and 614 GB of local storage
Delivers strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
Achieves 15.84 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks
Optimized for memory-intensive applications, databases, and caching layers
